item.title: Warsh Puts Stamp on Fed With Immediate Changes to Usual Routine
item.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh put his stamp on the US central bank Wednesday with a bevy of changes coming out of his first policy meeting, setting the stage for the wider overhaul of the institution he promised before he took the helm.

item.title: 【今朝の5本】仕事を始める前に読んでおきたい厳選ニュース
item.description: FOMC利上げ巡り予想割れる、イラン合意への批判一蹴、小泉防衛相、レアアース巡り合意、依存ゼロに

item.title: What a Missing Dot Revealed at the Fed
item.description: For more than a decade, officials at the US Federal Reserve have shared their predictions on future interest rates via a small scatter chart published four times a year. Nineteen central bank policymakers can offer their projections, reflected anonymously on the dot plot.

item.title: BOJ Will Hike Rates Again by December, Says 90% of Economists
item.description: An overwhelming majority of Bank of Japan watchers anticipate the central bank will raise interest rates again by the end of this year, following a hike to the highest level since 1995 earlier this week, according to a Bloomberg survey.

item.title: Oaktree Private Credit Fund Redemptions Drop Below Key 5% Limit
item.description: An Oaktree Capital Management private credit fund saw redemption requests drop by nearly half in the second quarter, making it the first major firm to stem a growing exodus from the $1.8 trillion industry.

item.title: Apple Prepares Second-Generation iPhone Air for Spring 2027
item.description: Apple Inc. is preparing a second-generation iPhone Air for spring 2027, aiming to boost the appeal of the slimmed-down device, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Trump Says He’d Rather Not Have USMCA, Teasing Termination
item.description: President Donald Trump said he would rather the US not be a part of the US-Mexico-Canada trilateral trade pact, but sent mixed signals about an upcoming renewal deadline and stopped short of threatening outright to quit.

item.title: Dollar Rallies as Fed Officials Open Door to 2026 Rate Hike
item.description: The dollar surged the most in three months after Federal Reserve officials signaled growing support for interest-rate hikes this year.

item.title: Yen Slides to Weakest Level Versus Dollar Since July 2024
item.description: The yen slumped to its weakest level against the US dollar since July 2024, raising the risk of official intervention to prop it up.

item.title: Warsh Forms Task Force to Review Fed’s $6.7 Trillion Balance Sheet
item.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh said he is appointing a task force to examine the central bank’s $6.7 trillion balance sheet, a first step in addressing a policy issue he has long criticized.

item.title: UK Cabinet Pushes Starmer to Increase Defense Spending Amid Row
item.description: Some UK cabinet ministers are pushing Prime Minister Keir Starmer and his chancellor of the Exchequer, Rachel Reeves, to increase defense funding further, following John Healey’s resignation over the spending row.

item.title: JPMorgan’s Michele Says Fed Market Message Is to Brace for Hikes
item.description: JPMorgan Asset Management’s Bob Michele expressed surprise at the hawkish tilt from the Federal Open Market Committee under new Chairman Kevin Warsh, saying policymakers sense more risk from inflation pressures than Wall Street had expected.

item.title: Bass Pro Shops Is Buying a Luxury Florida Keys Fishing Lodge
item.description: Outdoor retailer Bass Pro Shops is buying a historic fishing lodge in the Florida Keys, adding another luxury hotel to its growing portfolio.

item.title: US Yields Jump as Fed Dot Plot Boosts Trader Bets on a 2026 Rate Hike
item.description: US Treasury yields leaped as Federal Reserve officials signaled they expect an interest-rate hike in the coming months, pushing traders to fully price in higher borrowing costs in 2026.

item.title: Stellantis Is in Talks With Two Possible Partners for Maserati
item.description: Stellantis NV Chief Executive Officer Antonio Filosa said the company is in talks with two potential partners for Maserati, which will have implications for the plants producing the ailing luxury’s brand’s cars.

item.title: The Fed’s New Dot Plot After Its June Policy Meeting
item.description: The Federal Reserve’s so-called dot plot, which the US central bank uses to signal its outlook for the path of interest rates, shows the year-end 2026 median projection for the federal funds rate at 3.750%. Fed policymakers on Wednesday kept their benchmark rate steady in Kevin Warsh’s first meeting as Fed chair.

item.title: Fed Holds Rates Steady, Officials Split Over Hikes This Year
item.description: Federal Reserve officials left interest rates unchanged and were split over whether they expect to raise rates this year.

item.title: Key Takeaways From Fed Decision to Hold Rates Steady
item.description: Here are key takeaways from the Federal Reserve’s interest-rate decision on Wednesday:

item.title: Maybe the Market Was Never Expensive
item.description: If only we had known what was coming.

item.title: AI Health Startup Wants to Assist Half of Latin American Doctors
item.description: An Andreessen Horowitz-backed healthcare startup born in Latin America wants to put its AI assistant in the hands of half the region’s 1.9 million doctors by the end of 2027, a bet that technology can help bridge a shortage of medical professionals across strained health systems.

item.title: A First-Ever Default Shakes an $80 Billion Corner of Muni Market
item.description: More than two decades after Wall Street started pumping out a new type of bonds — those backed by the legal-settlement payments governments receive from cigarette companies — one batch has finally been driven into a default. It almost certainly won’t be the last.

item.title: Operar día y noche: el cambio cultural que se aproxima a Wall Street
item.description: Las entidades financieras tradicionales están empezando a evaluar modelos de negociación ininterrumpida de diversos activos, de una forma que lleva años revolucionando los horarios en el sector cripto.

item.title: Capital Factory CEO Killed in Private-Jet Crash in Texas
item.description: Joshua Baer, the founder and chief executive officer of the venture capital firm Capital Factory, died Tuesday in a plane crash in South Texas, the Austin American-Statesman reported.

item.title: Wall Street Revives Risky Loan Deals That Banks Couldn’t Sell
item.description: Wall Street banks are looking to make the most of rising demand for risky debt with a raft of leveraged loan sales that they previously couldn’t sell.

item.title: Brazilian Nickel Seeks Anchor Investor for $1.4 Billion Mine
item.description: Brazilian Nickel Ltd. is seeking an anchor investor to help attract more equity funding for its planned $1.4 billion nickel and cobalt mine in northeastern Brazil.

item.title: India’s Largest Bourse NSE Files Draft Papers for Landmark IPO
item.description: National Stock Exchange of India Ltd., the operator of the world’s busiest derivatives market, has filed draft documents for what’s on track to be one of the largest initial public offerings in India’s history.

item.title: Brevan Howard Prepares to Start Backing External Stocks Traders
item.description: Brevan Howard Asset Management is preparing to give money to stocks-focused hedge funds, adding to a wave of giant firms forking over cash to outside talent.

item.title: Fed Expected to Hold Rates in Warsh's First Decision
item.description: Kevin Warsh Photographer: Graeme Sloan/Bloomberg

item.title: Russian Gasoline Prices Skyrocket as Ukraine Strikes Refineries
item.description: Russian gasoline prices advanced further, with drivers in the summer vacation season facing headaches after some filling stations across the nation limited sales due to Ukrainian attacks on refineries.

item.title: Corn Ships Cross Hormuz Toward Iran, UAE Ahead of Trump Deal
item.description: A fully-loaded corn ship bound for Iran entered the Persian Gulf after crossing the US blockade line outside the Strait of Hormuz, ahead of the US-Iran interim peace deal aimed at reopening the waterway.
